reasoning: The 1-year Treasury bill rate is at 3.45% as of late October 2025. The general macroeconomic environment in late 2024 to 2025 has been a rate-cutting cycle by the Federal Reserve, exerting downward pressure on short-term treasury yields. For the very near term (2025-11-16), the continuation of this momentum makes an increase less likely (42%), aligned with the base rate for FRED indicators. As the forecast horizon extends to December 2025 and February 2026, uncertainty regarding future macroeconomic shocks, inflation rebounding, or the end of the Fed's cutting cycle grows. Following instructions, the probabilities are drawn closer to 0.5 to reflect this growing uncertainty over more distant horizons.
